title: Injection vulnerabilities - SonarQube for IDE Docs - VS Code
content:
# Injection vulnerabilities

## [Prerequisites](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/taint-vulnerabilities/#prerequisites "Prerequisites")

- Running SonarQube for IDE in connected mode with SonarQube Cloud or a SonarQube Server commercial edition is required. See the pages on [Connected mode](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/team-features/connected-mode/ "Connected mode") and [Connected mode setup](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/team-features/connected-mode-setup/ "Connected mode setup") for more details.
- Your project should be analyzed regularly, ideally by your CI server.

### [Limitations](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/taint-vulnerabilities/#limitations "Limitations")

- Only taint vulnerabilities in open files are shown in the IDE.
- SonarQube for IDE does not support short-lived branches therefore, when running in connected mode with SonarQube Cloud, you must work with [long-lived branches](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/branch-analysis-setup/#long-lived-and-short-lived-branches "long-lived branches"); SonarQube Server does not distinguish between long- and short-lived branches. 

  - SonarQube for VS Code follows [these methods to choose which branch](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/team-features/connected-mode/#branch-awareness "these methods to choose which branch") (in SonarQube (Server, Cloud)) to sync with its local analysis, including the synchronization of taint vulnerabilities in your IDE.
- You’re limited by your SonarQube (Server, Cloud) plan restrictions regarding lines of code limits and branches you can analyze.

## [How to display taint vulnerabilities](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/taint-vulnerabilities/#how-to-display-taint-vulnerabilities "How to display taint vulnerabilities")

1. Set up [Connected mode](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/team-features/connected-mode-setup/ "Connected mode") and bind your project to SonarQube (Server, Cloud).

2. In the standard VS Code Panel below the editor region, select the **PROBLEMS** panel.

3. Along with regular issues, the **PROBLEMS** panel displays the list of injection vulnerabilities that are found in the bound folder. SonarQube for VS Code displays injection vulnerabilities for the entire project.

## [How to fix your taint vulnerabilities](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/taint-vulnerabilities/#how-to-fix-your-taint-issues "How to fix your taint vulnerabilities")

Injection vulnerabilities are distinguished in the **PROBLEMS** panel with the *Latest SonarQube Server/SonarQube Cloud Analysis* tag as shown in the following image. Learn how to fix your injection vulnerability by using the tooltip options:

1. Select **View as Table** for a more detailed view of your issues.

2. In the **PROBLEMS** view, your taint vulnerabilities are easily identifiable by looking at the analysis **Source**.

3. Select one of your taint vulnerabilities open the **SONARQUBE ISSUE LOCATIONS** view.

4. Click on the lightbulb or right-click on the issue shown to select **Show issue details**; it will open the **SonarQube Rule Description**.

5. Find more information under the **How can I fix it?** tab.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![Injection vulnerabilities (also known as taint vulnerabilities) are also shown in the PROBLEMS view.](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/5d394c74-766b-4fd3-a81c-471af90483f7/sq-vscode-fixing-injection-vulnerabilities.png?w=3456&h=2234&auto=format&fit=crop "Injection vulnerabilities (also known as taint vulnerabilities) are also shown in the PROBLEMS view.")

Because the detection of taint vulnerabilities requires that you run in connected mode, any changes you make to the code must be analyzed by your instance of SonarQube (Server, Cloud). Here are two options to resolve taint vulnerabilities displayed by SonarQube for IDE:

- After you fix the taint vulnerability in your IDE, commit your code to the server and rerun the analysis on SonarQube (Server, Cloud). The new status (of the vulnerability) will be reflected in your IDE.
- Go to the issue in SonarQube (Server, Cloud) and mark it as **Accept** or **False positive**. The new status will be updated locally in less than one minute.

When running in connected mode with SonarQube Server 10.4 or newer, **Won’t Fix** becomes **Accept**.

See the [Fixing issues](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/fixing-issues/ "Fixing issues") page for complete details about fixing issues, including [injection and taint vulnerabilities](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/fixing-issues/#fixing-taint-vulnerabilities "injection and taint vulnerabilities"), in SonarQube for VS Code.
